page_content:
BUSM788 Global Business Experience (1-6 Credits) Provides MBA students the opportunity to combine classroom learning and project research with seminars in a host country. The focus and locations visited will vary. Restriction: Must be in Business and Management (Master's) program; or permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business. Repeatable to: 8 credits if content differs.

BUSO604 Managerial Economics and Public Policy (2 Credits) Basic microeconomic principles used by ﬁrms, including supply and demand, elasticities, costs, productivity, pricing, market structure and competitive implications of alternative market structures. Market failures and government intervention. Public policy processes affecting business operations. Restriction: Must be in Business and Management (Master's) program; or permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business. Credit Only Granted for: EMBA681 or BUSO604.

Courses in 2025-2026 Academic Catalog - DRAFT COPY

BUSO612 Marketing Management (2 Credits) Analysis of marketing problems and evaluation of speciﬁc marketing efforts regarding the organization's products and services, pricing activities, channel selection, and promotion strategies in both domestic and international markets. Restriction: Must be in Business and Management (Master's) program; or permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business. Credit Only Granted for: EMBA757 or BUSO612.
